response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack generalist with 15+ years spanning web, mobile, and embedded software, but the resume contains no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, or deep-learning workload performance analysis — the core technical pillars of this principal-level role. Some low-level C/C++ and DLang work demonstrates systems programming aptitude, and a recent AI-agent infrastructure role shows adjacent domain awareness, but neither bridges the gap to hardware-software co-design for AI accelerators.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience including C/C++ and DLang systems work, showing some capacity for low-level programming
Prior role involved building low-latency services handling millions of requests per second using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases, demonstrating performance-oriented thinking
Recent contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP-based agentic frameworks indicates growing familiarity with the AI ecosystem
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD experience — the primary technical requirements of this role are entirely absent from the resume
Career trajectory is firmly rooted in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), not hardware optimization or deep-learning performance engineering
Holds a Bachelor's degree with no advanced degree in a relevant engineering or CS discipline, a meaningful gap for a principal-level hardware research role that strongly prefers MS or PhD candidates

key_factors.factor: No deep-learning workload performance optimization background
Low-level systems programming exposure (C/C++, DLang, async I/O)
